'''Cortosis-weave armor''' was an [[Armor/Legends|armor]] which was resistant to [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. It contained traces of [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]], an ore prized for its ability to create an energy feedback loop which would temporarily shut down a lightsaber.

As cortosis weave contained only trace amounts of the ore woven into it, the armor lost the ability to short out an opponent's energy blade. Instead armor containing the cortosis weave would merely be resistant to lightsaber strikes.

Cortosis weave was in use as early as [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]] as both an armor underlay and for the tempering of traditional melee weapons against lightsabers. At this time cortosis ore seemed to be much more common, later, however it would become a rarer, more valuable material that would give the user an unexpected edge against a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|force-sensitive]] opponent.

The armor was known to have been used by the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] in the [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] [[Yinchorri Uprising]]. A variation was also used by the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] fought in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Susevfi]]. The Jensaarai customized their armor with cortosis weave patterns for decoration, although it retained the more pragmatic abilities of the armor. The cortosis used in their armor was smuggled from the gas planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. [[Shadowtrooper]] armor worn by the force sensitive troops of the [[Empire Reborn]] battled by [[Kyle Katarn]] and the members of the [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Jedi academy]] in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]] also contained cortosis weave.
